key_insight: Scratch testing is a fast but method-sensitive way to judge adhesion and integrity of thermally sprayed coatings, with results strongly shaped by microstructure, residual stress, and porosity.
engineering_relevance: For aerospace, energy, automotive, and industrial coatings, better scratch-test interpretation can improve coating qualification, failure diagnosis, and process optimization.
method: Review and compare scratch-test methodologies for thermally sprayed coatings
Relate scratch response to coating architecture, residual stress, porosity, and failure mode
Summarize critical-load evaluation using microscopy, acoustic emission, and multi-axis force data
evidence_signal: The paper synthesizes findings across many coating systems and thermal spray routes including APS, HVOF, HVAF, and D-gun to correlate scratch behavior with measured failure evolution.
watch_item: Scratch-test outcomes are not yet standardized, so critical load values can vary significantly with test setup, criteria, and interpretation.
paper_title: A critical analysis of the scratch test for thermally sprayed coatings
